description: Adversarial invariant for the manual video-endoscopy, laparoscopic-surgery, and surgical-robotics audit: procedure/device nodes should stay anchored to source-backed method edges rather than hospital IT.

checks.reason: Hospital information systems are not a video-endoscope device component.
Video endoscopy requires electronic image-sensor capture.
Fiberoptic endoscopy is predecessor/enabling context, not the electronic sensor itself.
Video endoscopy should stay anchored to the 1983 first video endoscope chronology.
Hospital information systems are not part of laparoscopic operative technique.
Modern laparoscopic surgery requires endoscopic operative visualization.
Aseptic surgery is clinical practice context rather than the video laparoscope component.
Modern laparoscopic surgery should stay anchored to the 1985 first laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
Laparoscopy is deployment context for surgical robotics, not a hard prerequisite for all surgical robots.
Surgical robotics should stay anchored to the 1985 PUMA 560 robot-assisted surgery chronology.
